hypothesis: Incorporating a hierarchical reward function in reinforcement learning that explicitly penalizes generated molecules whose lattice node representations fail symmetry and conjugation criteria—even when the corresponding substrate representations pass all filters—will yield a higher percentage of CTF candidates satisfying material-specific constraints than reward functions defined independently at the substrate level, because the mesh-level structural requirements impose constraints on substrate design that are not recoverable through post-hoc filtering of substrate-only optimized molecules.
